title: Public Records Request — Minnesota Department of Health Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Public Records Request — Minnesota Department of Health Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 24, 2026

Data Practices Compliance Official
Minnesota Department of Health
St. Paul, MN

Dear Data Practices Compliance Official,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Minnesota Department of Health's use of generative AI tools by agency employees.

We are requesting the following records under Minn. Stat. § 13.03, subd. 3:

1. All interaction logs from Microsoft Copilot Chat, ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by MDH employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from April 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing MDH employee use of Microsoft Copilot Chat or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The Minnesota Department of Health oversees public health policy, disease surveillance, health regulation, and licensing. AI tools may be used for policy analysis, regulatory drafting, epidemiological research, and public communications. MNIT confirmed that Copilot usage data is held by each agency individually, and we are directing this request to MDH for its own employees' usage.

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Microsoft Copilot interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Under the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, all government data collected, created, received, maintained, or disseminated by a government entity is presumed public unless classified otherwise by statute. Minn. Stat. §§ 13.01, 13.03. Governor Walz's Executive Order 24-03 (2024) directed state agencies to develop policies for responsible AI use including transparency and accountability. Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) rolled out Microsoft Copilot Chat to executive branch employees in April 2025 as part of the state's existing Microsoft contract (see GovTech, https://www.govtech.com/biz/minnesota-taps-free-genai-through-microsoft-partnership). MNIT has confirmed that Copilot usage data for individual agencies is held by each agency, not centrally by MNIT. Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.

title: Public Records Request — Minnesota Department of Transportation Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Public Records Request — Minnesota Department of Transportation Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 24, 2026

Data Practices Compliance Official
Minnesota Department of Transportation
St. Paul, MN

Dear Data Practices Compliance Official,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Minnesota Department of Transportation's use of generative AI tools by agency employees.

We are requesting the following records under Minn. Stat. § 13.03, subd. 3:

1. All interaction logs from Microsoft Copilot Chat, ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by MnDOT employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from April 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

The Minnesota Department of Transportation manages the state's transportation infrastructure, including highways, bridges, and transit systems. Similar records have been successfully obtained from the Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), which disclosed AI usage records in response to a public records request (see MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files). I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Microsoft Copilot interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Under the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, all government data collected, created, received, maintained, or disseminated by a government entity is presumed public unless classified otherwise by statute. Minn. Stat. §§ 13.01, 13.03. Governor Walz's Executive Order 24-03 (2024) directed state agencies to develop policies for responsible AI use including transparency and accountability. Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) rolled out Microsoft Copilot Chat to executive branch employees in April 2025 as part of the state's existing Microsoft contract (see GovTech, https://www.govtech.com/biz/minnesota-taps-free-genai-through-microsoft-partnership). St. Paul, MN

We are writing to request records related to Office of the Governor of Minnesota's use of generative AI tools by agency employees.

We are requesting the following records under Minn. Stat. § 13.03, subd. 3:

1. Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Microsoft Copilot interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Under the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, all government data collected, created, received, maintained, or disseminated by a government entity is presumed public unless classified otherwise by statute. Minn. Stat. §§ 13.01, 13.03. Governor Walz's Executive Order 24-03 (2024) directed state agencies to develop policies for responsible AI use including transparency and accountability. Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) rolled out Microsoft Copilot Chat to executive branch employees in April 2025 as part of the state's existing Microsoft contract (see GovTech, https://www.govtech.com/biz/minnesota-taps-free-genai-through-microsoft-partnership). title: Public Records Request — Minnesota Management and Budget Generative AI Records

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Public Records Request — Minnesota Management and Budget Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
Subject: Public Records Request — AI Chatbot Interaction Logs

April 24, 2026

Data Practices Compliance Official
Minnesota Management and Budget
St. Paul, MN

Dear Data Practices Compliance Official,

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

We are writing to request records related to Minnesota Management and Budget's use of generative AI tools by agency employees.

We are requesting the following records under Minn. Stat. § 13.03, subd. 3:

1. All interaction logs from Microsoft Copilot Chat, ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Claude, or any other generative AI chatbot tool — including prompts submitted by MMB employees and responses generated by the AI — for the period from April 1, 2025 through the date this request is processed. We request these records in their native electronic format where available (e.g., HTML exports, JSON, or CSV).

2. Any policies, guidelines, acceptable use policies, or training materials governing MMB employee use of Microsoft Copilot Chat or other generative AI chatbot tools.

3. Any data retention or deletion policies applicable to AI chatbot interaction logs, including any schedules for automatic deletion of interaction data.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

Minnesota Management and Budget oversees the state's fiscal operations, budget development, and enterprise procurement. As the agency responsible for statewide financial planning and resource allocation, MMB employees are well-positioned to use generative AI tools for budget analysis, procurement review, and policy drafting. MNIT confirmed that Copilot usage data is held by each agency individually, and we are directing this request to MMB for its own employees' usage.

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/; see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files.

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

I understand that this may be a novel type of records request and I am happy to answer any technical questions. Please feel free to contact me at the email address below.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

Sincerely,
Alexander Spangher
Postdoctoral Researcher
Stanford University
alexspan@stanford.edu

P.S. To facilitate your response, we note that Microsoft Copilot interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console or the Purview compliance center. ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

P.P.S. Under the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, all government data collected, created, received, maintained, or disseminated by a government entity is presumed public unless classified otherwise by statute. Minn. Stat. §§ 13.01, 13.03. Governor Walz's Executive Order 24-03 (2024) directed state agencies to develop policies for responsible AI use including transparency and accountability. Minnesota IT Services (MNIT) rolled out Microsoft Copilot Chat to executive branch employees in April 2025 as part of the state's existing Microsoft contract (see GovTech, https://www.govtech.com/biz/minnesota-taps-free-genai-through-microsoft-partnership). MNIT has confirmed that Copilot usage data for individual agencies is held by each agency, not centrally by MNIT. Additionally, NARA Bulletin 2023-02 (Jan. 5, 2023), https://www.archives.gov/records-mgmt/bulletins/2023/2023-02, provides persuasive authority that chats and electronic messages used for government business are likely government records.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FOIA US Navy Vietnam 4/23
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records for subject below;
Name: Michael Trigg
Branch: United States Navy
DOB: April 1, 1949
Dates of Service: Approx 1966 to 1975

Request following documents:
1) hard copy of Bronze Star Medal award citation
2) History of Assignments NAVPERS form 601-5
3) Navy commendation medal award citation
5) Presidential Unit Citation on file
Historical Documentation, Vietnam War history

I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information.

Please mail hardcopies to:
MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115
or please email pdf copies to requests@muckrock.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley
